mnemonics: I/O Methods Can Be Very Polling: Interrupt-driven, Memory-mapped, Channel I/O, Bus-based DMA, Vector Interrupts, Polling
keyPoints: I/O devices can be accessed via Programmed I/O (Polling), Interrupt-driven I/O, or Direct Memory Access (DMA).
Memory-mapped I/O uses the same address space for memory and devices, while Port-mapped I/O uses a separate, dedicated address space.
The primary goal of efficient I/O is to offload work from the CPU to specialized controllers to improve overall system performance.
commonMistakes: Confusing Memory-Mapped I/O with Port-Mapped I/O (Isolated I/O).
Assuming Polling is always worse than Interrupt-Driven I/O; Polling can be more efficient for high-speed devices or simple systems with predictable timing.
